mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) signals expanding supply capacity and robust economic activity, which leads to higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward rank IC of INDPRO level on 60-day forward returns remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or the sign of the average return in the top quartile of INDPRO is negative.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 5.5% indicates aggressive monetary tightening that reduces liquidity and raises discount rates, compressing equity valuations and driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: In more than 50% of instances where FEDFUNDS >5.5, the S&P 500 5-day return is positive, or the average excess return over cash in such periods is not statistically negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in both housing starts and industrial production (3-month positive changes) signals synchronized expansion in construction and manufacturing, which boosts aggregate demand and corporate profits, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: In periods with both HOUST and INDPRO rising over 3 months, the S&P 500 60-day return is positive in less than 55% of cases, or the mean return is not significantly positive (one-sided t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5% (recession threshold), rising retail sales signal a consumption-led recovery that boosts earnings and equity prices, but this effect is regime-dependent and only active during high-unemployment periods.
failure_condition: In periods with UNRATE >5%, the correlation between RSAFS growth and 20-day forward returns is negative or zero, or the average return in months with RSAFS growth >0 is not higher than in months with negative growth (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP (GDPC1) reflects stronger overall economic output and corporate profitability, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over the following 5 trading days as investors quickly price in the robust growth.
failure_condition: If the 5-day forward return after GDPC1 releases is positive less than 55% of the time, or the rank IC of GDPC1 on 5-day returns is below 0.03 over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5%, it signals a weakening labor market that will reduce consumer spending and corporate earnings, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In more than 50% of months with UNRATE >5%, the S&P 500 20-day return is positive, or the mean return is not statistically negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When both consumer spending (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) decline month-over-month, it signals a broad contraction in demand that lowers corporate revenues and drives negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In months where both RSAFS and HOUST decline, the S&P 500 20-day return is positive in more than 50% of cases, or the average return is not negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: In a high inflation environment (CPIAUCSL above 205), rising industrial production signals an overheating economy that will prompt central bank tightening, which depresses equity valuations and produces negative returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: In periods when CPIAUCSL >205, the correlation between INDPRO growth and 20-day forward returns is positive, or the average return in top INDPRO growth quartile is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10).
